page: Amazon Web Services c5d.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed, low-latency local instance store capacity. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The hardware configuration does not include any dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on the Intel Xeon CPU for processing workloads.

In benchmark evaluations, the c5d.12xlarge demonstrates balanced performance. It achieves a Passmark CPU score of 39050.61 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 47275.72, both falling within the average range. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a Passmark memory score of 2767.33, placing it in the top 25% tier. Large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 115.58 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 48.85 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance handles 41.07 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 16994.18 on Passmark database operations. However, it exhibits limitations in large-scale AI tasks, delivering a weak prompt processing speed of 6.59 tokens/sec for a 70B LLM model.

The c5d.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high compute density coupled with fast local storage. By bundling 1800 GB of NVMe SSD storage directly with dedicated compute resources, it eliminates the need for external block storage for temporary data. This makes the instance well-suited for compute-intensive applications, high-performance web serving, database operations, batch processing, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance designed for high-performance workloads requiring fast local storage. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a 24-core Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 48 vCPUs running at 3.6 GHz, supported by 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmarks, the c5d.12xlarge delivers strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% with a Passmark memory score of 2767.33, alongside solid database processing capabilities. However, its lack of dedicated GPUs results in weak performance for large-scale language model inference. This instance is highly suitable for compute-heavy applications, database operations, and software compilation tasks that benefit from high-speed local instance storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong memory and database performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database and processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed.
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong top-tier benchmark performance.
1800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for compute-intensive applications, software compilation, and database workloads.
